purpose: Bring the new-project Autopilot propose/execute pipeline into a working deterministic baseline by adding a TemplateProposeHandler for coding/new_software_project tasks. The handler must generate executable command/tool_calls without relying on sgpt/LM Studio structured tool output.
problem_statement: The new-project Autopilot creates tasks, but the propose step fails with autopilot_no_executable_step. The current propose flow calls sgpt, which talks to LM Studio without a tools parameter. The model returns unstructured text. parse_structured_action_payload cannot parse command/tool_calls, so no executable step is produced.
current_status_notes: Model routing was fixed so default model auto is tried before benchmark models.
/project-workspaces/ was created because the Docker bind mount was missing.
Hub was started with ROLE=hub instead of the previous default worker role.
Fibonacci API was manually created and tests pass 10/10.
Remaining target: make the Hub/Autopilot produce and execute steps automatically, not manually.
architecture_decision: For the first working slice, implement deterministic handler-based propose for known task kinds. This is not the final artifact-first architecture, but it removes the fragile dependency on sgpt/LM Studio returning structured JSON/tool calls. Later artifact-first work can replace/extend this path.
global_principles: Propose must return an executable command or tool_calls for supported task kinds.
Known template/new-project tasks should not require an LLM call to produce the first executable step.
Unsupported task kinds must return None and fall back cleanly to the existing propose flow.
Handler output must match TaskScopedRouteResponse expectations and must be usable by execute via last_proposal.
The handler must respect available tools/allowed_tools and not invent tools that are unavailable.
E2E success is measured by ananta new-project generating a real Fibonacci API workspace and tests passing.
non_goals: Do not solve full artifact-first manifest completion in this todo.
Do not require LM Studio tool-calling support for this path.
Do not parse unstructured LLM text as the primary propose result.
Do not bypass existing task-scoped execution safety checks.
Do not make the handler catch all unknown task kinds. Unknown kinds must fall back.

milestones.title: Understand handler contracts and available tools
Validate propose and execute in Hub
End-to-end new-project validation and fallback behavior

tasks.title: Analyze TaskHandlerRegistry and handler protocol
tasks.goal: Fully understand how handlers are registered and how _try_handler_propose dispatches propose calls.
acceptance_criteria: agent/services/task_handler_registry.py is reviewed and the TaskHandler protocol/signature is documented in notes or test comments.
DeterministicRepairHandler in agent/ai_agent.py is identified as the registration reference.
The existing register_task_handler() call in ai_agent.py is located and understood.
_try_handler_propose() in task_scoped_execution_service.py is reviewed and expected handler return shapes are documented.
TaskScopedRouteResponse structure for handler responses is identified, including fields used by propose/execute.
implementation_notes: Do this before coding to avoid returning a shape that propose accepts but execute ignores.
Record exact expected keys for command/tool_calls/reason/metadata if not already obvious.
test_requirements: No mandatory code test, but later handler tests must prove compatibility with _try_handler_propose.

tasks.title: Analyze SDK/Shell tool registry and allowed_tools handling
tasks.goal: Understand which tool definitions are available to the handler and how allowed_tools constrains propose output.
acceptance_criteria: tool_definitions_resolver path, likely tool_registry.get_tool_definitions, is identified and can be called from the handler or tests.
The structure of returned tool definitions is documented: tool name, parameters, required fields and execution mapping.
Availability of common tools such as write_file, read_file and bash/command is verified or their actual names are documented.
normalize_allowed_tools behavior is understood: how task context restricts tool usage and how unavailable tools should be handled.
Handler design includes a fallback if write_file is unavailable but bash is available.
implementation_notes: Do not hardcode tool names without verifying actual registry names where possible.
The handler should prefer safer file-write tools over shell heredoc when available.
test_requirements: Add unit fixture with fake tool_definitions_resolver exposing write_file and bash variants.

tasks.title: Implement TemplateProposeHandler
tasks.goal: Create a deterministic TaskHandler for task_kind coding and new_software_project that returns executable proposal steps without an LLM call.
acceptance_criteria: agent/handlers/template_propose_handler.py exists.
TemplateProposeHandler implements the TaskHandler protocol with propose(self, **kwargs) and execute(self, **kwargs).
propose() returns TaskScopedRouteResponse(data={...}) or the exact accepted dict shape used by _try_handler_propose.
For supported task kinds, propose() returns either command as a non-empty string or tool_calls as a non-empty list of dicts.
For task_kind='new_software_project', propose() can produce deterministic file creation steps for a small Python/Flask Fibonacci API project when the task description indicates that goal.
For task_kind='coding', propose() produces a safe generic command/tool_calls proposal only when the task description is recognized enough to be deterministic.
Handler uses tool_definitions_resolver to inspect available tools and does not emit unavailable tools if avoidable.
Handler respects allowed_tools from kwargs/context. If write_file is not allowed but bash is allowed, it may emit a bash heredoc command. If neither is allowed, it returns None with a reason.
Handler sets reason, for example 'TemplateProposeHandler: write_file app.py requirements.txt README.md tests'.
execute() returns None so the existing execute pipeline uses last_proposal/default execution flow, unless the protocol requires another pass-through shape.
implementation_notes: Keep the first template deliberately narrow: Python Flask Fibonacci API with app.py, requirements.txt and tests/README if requested.
Prefer multiple write_file tool_calls when the tool exists. If only shell is available, emit one deterministic shell command that creates files using cat > file <<'EOF'.
Do not call sgpt/generate_text/LM Studio inside this handler.
Do not mark execution completed inside propose. Propose only suggests executable step(s).
test_requirements: Unit test: new_software_project + write_file tools returns tool_calls.
Unit test: new_software_project + only bash allowed returns command.
Unit test: unknown task_kind returns None.
Unit test: unsupported/unsafe description returns None or safe fallback, not random shell.
anti_patterns: Do not emit free-text proposal without command/tool_calls.
Do not call the LLM from the deterministic handler.

tasks.title: Register TemplateProposeHandler in Hub app
tasks.goal: Register the handler for coding and new_software_project task kinds so _try_handler_propose uses it before falling back to sgpt/LLM propose.
acceptance_criteria: TemplateProposeHandler is imported in agent/ai_agent.py or an appropriate handler registration module.
register_task_handler('coding', handler, app=app, ...) is called with correct capabilities/safety flags.
register_task_handler('new_software_project', handler, app=app, ...) is called with correct capabilities/safety flags.
Registration occurs during Hub startup when ROLE=hub, alongside or near DeterministicRepairHandler registration.
Existing DeterministicRepairHandler registration continues to work.
Safety flags include explicit values such as requires_review=false or the project-standard equivalent, and no unsafe broad permissions are introduced.
Tests verify registry contains handlers for coding and new_software_project after app setup.
implementation_notes: Either rename _register_deterministic_repair_handler() to a broader _register_task_handlers() or add a second registration function. Prefer minimal low-risk change.
Do not register the handler multiple times if app factory is called repeatedly.
test_requirements: Registration test using app factory or registry fixture.
anti_patterns: Do not hide registration behind worker role only.
Do not remove deterministic repair handler.

tasks.title: Validate handler propose path in running Hub
tasks.goal: Prove POST /tasks/<tid>/step/propose uses _try_handler_propose and returns an executable step.
acceptance_criteria: Hub health-check shows role: hub in the test/manual run.
A coding or new_software_project task can be created manually or via goal submit.
POST /tasks/<tid>/step/propose returns HTTP success and includes command or tool_calls.
Response includes reason from TemplateProposeHandler.
Logs or response metadata show handler_contract/task_kind and handler selection.
_try_handler_propose returns the handler response and does not fall through to _propose_single_task_step for supported tasks.
No autopilot_no_executable_step occurs for the supported Fibonacci/new-project task.
implementation_notes: If the API currently hides handler metadata, add safe debug metadata only when appropriate.
Prefer automated service-level test over manual-only verification.
test_requirements: Service/API test for handler propose path.

tasks.title: Validate execute pipeline consumes handler proposal
tasks.goal: Ensure a proposed command/tool_calls from TemplateProposeHandler is actually executed via the existing execute path.
acceptance_criteria: POST /tasks/<tid>/step/execute without explicit command uses last_proposal.command or last_proposal.tool_calls.
Execution creates expected files in the task workspace.
Exit code is 0 for the deterministic Fibonacci template proposal.
Task status transitions according to existing execute rules and does not fail with no executable step.
Workspace snapshot shows expected files such as app.py, requirements.txt and tests or README as defined by the handler.
If execution fails, error is a real command/tool error, not missing proposal structure.
implementation_notes: This test may reveal whether execute currently supports tool_calls from last_proposal or only command. If tool_calls are not supported, either adapt handler to emit command first or add tool_calls execution support as a separate patch.
test_requirements: Integration test: propose then execute using handler output.

tasks.title: Run ananta new-project end-to-end for Fibonacci API
tasks.goal: Validate the full Autopilot path creates a real Fibonacci API project through Hub orchestration, not manual file creation.
acceptance_criteria: ananta new-project 'Python Fibonacci API mit REST-Endpunkt' or equivalent test entrypoint creates a Goal.
Autoplanner creates tasks for the project.
Task propose produces valid executable steps via TemplateProposeHandler for supported task kind(s).
Task execute runs the proposed steps.
Workspace under /project-workspaces/fibonacci or test workspace contains app.py, requirements.txt and tests/README as expected.
pytest or equivalent project test command passes.
No manual file creation or manual task status patch is required.
No autopilot_no_executable_step occurs in the E2E flow.
implementation_notes: Use isolated temp workspace in automated tests if possible; keep /project-workspaces only for manual/docker validation.
This can be a smoke E2E first, then hardened later with artifact-first manifest checks.
test_requirements: E2E or integration test for new-project Fibonacci flow.

tasks.title: Ensure unsupported task kinds fall back safely
tasks.goal: Unknown or unsupported task kinds must not crash and must not block the legacy propose fallback.
acceptance_criteria: TemplateProposeHandler.propose returns None for unsupported task_kind.
_try_handler_propose returns None when the handler declines.
The existing _propose_single_task_step fallback still runs after handler decline.
No exception/crash occurs for unknown task_kind.
Logs/metadata indicate handler_declined or unsupported_task_kind for diagnostics.
implementation_notes: This keeps the deterministic handler narrow and safe.
test_requirements: Unit/service test for unsupported task_kind fallback.

tasks.title: Document current deterministic propose baseline and relation to artifact-first work
tasks.goal: Make clear that TemplateProposeHandler is the immediate deterministic baseline, while artifact-first manifests remain the longer-term robust output/completion architecture.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain why sgpt/LM Studio unstructured output caused autopilot_no_executable_step.
Docs explain TemplateProposeHandler flow: task_kind -> handler -> command/tool_calls -> last_proposal -> execute.
Docs list supported task kinds and explicit unsupported fallback behavior.
Docs reference future artifact-first handoff/completion work without mixing both scopes.
This todo JSON is updated after implementation slices or a status companion file is created.

definition_of_done_global: TemplateProposeHandler exists and is registered for coding and new_software_project.
Supported task kinds produce executable command or tool_calls without LLM call.
POST /tasks/<tid>/step/propose returns executable proposal and no longer fails with autopilot_no_executable_step for Fibonacci/new-project task.
POST /tasks/<tid>/step/execute consumes last_proposal and creates expected project files.
ananta new-project Fibonacci API works through Hub orchestration and tests pass.
Unsupported task kinds fall back cleanly to existing propose flow.
Tests cover handler propose, registration, execute consumption, E2E Fibonacci and fallback behavior.
summary_notes: This is the pragmatic immediate fix for autopilot_no_executable_step.
It intentionally avoids requiring LM Studio tool-calling or structured JSON output.
It should be implemented before the larger artifact-first handoff/completion track if the goal is to get new-project working quickly.
